entry.title: Could not split statements; SQL console content is too large
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

What is the issue and how to resolve it?

Error: Could not split statements; SQL console content is too large

Error Description:
Statement 'alter PROCEDURE SBO_SP_TransactionNotification ( in object_type nvarchar(30), -- SBO Object Type in ...'
successfully executed in 203 ms 183 µs (server processing time: 201 ms 195 µs) - Rows Affected: 0

@SapB1 @hanastudio @transaction_notification

Regards,

Mucip:)

entry.title: What is Composable Architecture?
entry.content:
So, what is Composable Business Architecture? Is this yet another "buzzword", or this is genuine approach to the Enterprise Architecture, Gartner is talking about[1]?

How is this related with Decoupled Integration (Architecture)?

And how is all this related with Event-Driven Architecture?

My 2 cents…

It’s a kind of buzzword, yes, not necessarily Gartner invented it – but it is meaningful one, to be honest….

Gartner talks about Composable Business (Architecture) in fact[1] – listing 4 main principles:

  • More speed through discovery
  • Greater agility through modularity
  • Better leadership through orchestration
  • Resilience through autonomy

If we go deeper – in the essence, it’s about (Architecture) building blocks supporting different business operations – which are connected seamlessly with each other. Of course, blocks are replaceable by other blocks (i.e. other products) supporting the same business operations or extending it. Actually, one may say nothing new that TOGAF is already not teaching us to do, as part of ADM[2]…

Sounds good? Well, maybe I am over-simplifying – but this is basically it...

We are talking about (Architecture) building blocks, which are not just built in any "best fit" style, but built in such a way to support agility, flexibility and resilience… And this is the main differentiator (and the main benefit, as well) of the modern Compossible Business (Architecture).

Clearly, Decoupled Integration (Architecture) is an enabling layer for the Composable Architecture. To put this in a very simple words (again), integrating via middleware and using API/Event Portal(s) is just must. What kind of "flexibility" would it be, if we would design P2P integrations between 2 building blocks? Probably none...

So, is Event-Driven Architecture answer to all?

Well, not really.

Event-Driven Architecture (EDA) is indeed one of the key Integration Styles providing Decoupled Integration. EDA, in its core, is built to be “decoupled”, but it is primarily executed asynchronously – although we are more and more talking about (near) real time integration as well[3] (I do strongly recommend this article in Solace from Bruno Baloi - pls note, when we talk about Solace PubSub+, we are also talking about SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh)

Fully synchronous integration, like REST or OData APIs can also be built through integration middleware (i.e. SAP Integration Suite incl. API Management) – let’s use Omni-channel integration as an example – where multiple commerce channels through the integration middleware are taking (in both synchronous and asynchronous fashion) orders on the same backend[4][5].

EDA is a key substance of the modern Decoupled Integration (Architecture) and thus of the Compossible Business (Architecture) – but we are not limited to EDA only… We should not abandon other good patterns[6] – as not one size fits all...

Final…

Those are my 2 cents…

Of course, would be glad to hear other thoughts as well…

In my next article(s) I will try to talk more about some practical examples (Events, APIs) and how those are addressing 4 principles of the modern Compossible Business.

Acknowledgment

*) Intro photo by Ryan Quintal on Unsplash

References

[1] The Future of Business Is Composable - Gartner Keynote

[2] Introduction to the ADM (opengroup.org)

[2] Why it's Time to Event Enable your Integration | Solace

[4] Building Integration Architecture for the Omni-cha... - SAP Community

[5] Part 3: Next steps in building the Integration Arc... - SAP Community

[6] Agile EA - from SOA to Interoperability - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content: My most favourite extensions.

entry.title: Re: Sap Webi Multivalue Error
entry.content:
- your screenshot does not start with the ID 🤔
- be sure that your ID is a dimension and not a measure
- be sure that you use the merged-ID-object in your table  
- try with min() or max() or first() or last() to avoid Multivalue
- start building your table column by column to find the problematic data and fix this column at first

entry.title: Re: continuous date range
entry.content:
you can try with min() or max() or first() or last() to find the requested dates
and you have to use the context of IN([Employee No]) in your formula to eliminate all other dimensions in your table

entry.title: Re: Can Azure AD be used in SAP Task Center config in pla...
entry.content: Im not an expert in entra id but you could connect it directly with BTP subaccount the same way the documentation describes it with ias: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/fundamentals/scenario-azure-first-sap-identity-integration . Instead of IAS you do this directly with your BTP subaccount. That should be the first step. Afterwords you should try to generate the global user id and provision it to your backend. Which provisioning tool do you use? This could be done with IPS.

entry.title: 🟠 Micro Frontends - What they are and how to implement them with Luigi
entry.content: In this talk, we’ll explore micro frontends - an architecture that breaks down large monolithic applications into smaller manageable pieces. Learn what micro frontends are and the benefits of using them across multiple teams with multiple tech preferences. There will also be a coding demo on getting started with Luigi and connecting several micro frontends.

entry.title: Re: What is Composable Architecture?
entry.content:
Hi Stevang , 

Thanks for sharing your insights on Composable Business Architecture! I agree that while it may seem like a buzzword, the principles from Gartner—speed, agility, leadership, and resilience—are crucial for modern enterprise architecture.

You’re right about Decoupled Integration being essential; using middleware and APIs enhances flexibility. EDA is indeed key, but it’s important to recognize that synchronous integration methods also play a vital role.

I look forward to your future articles on practical applications of these concepts!

Regards ,

Azmath.

entry.title: Re: New Member in the SAP Learning Community
entry.content:
Hi SMariaGray ,

Welcome to the SAP Learning Community! Your background in marketing and UX research will bring great insights as you explore SAP. I share your interest in customer experience, marketing, and supply chain, and I’d love to connect and exchange ideas!

Best of luck with your certification journey. Excited to learn and grow together!

Regards ,

Azmath.

entry.title: Re: SAP Concur - Managing Invoice Vendors via API
entry.content:
Hi Michael , 

Great post! Your insights on using Invoice APIs for real-time data integration are very helpful. The step-by-step guidance on managing vendor records will definitely streamline the Requisition-to-Payment process. Looking forward to the next installment!

Regards ,

entry.title: Re: SAP certificate renewal - practical guide
entry.content:
Hi ,

Great post! Thanks for outlining the essential steps for certificate renewal and the importance of careful planning to avoid communication failures and data loss. Your guidance on identifying partners, scheduling outages, and monitoring the renewal process is very practical. I especially appreciate the emphasis on communication with partners to ensure a smooth transition. This will definitely help many in managing their SAP integrations effectively!

Regards ,

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration: Advanced Event Mesh Adapter: OAuth [6]: using Microsoft Entra ID (Azure A
entry.content:
Hi ,

Thank you for sharing this detailed tutorial on setting up the Advanced Event Mesh Adapter with OAuth authentication! The step-by-step approach makes it easy to follow along, especially for those new to the SAP Cloud Integration environment. I appreciate the focus on Microsoft Entra ID for the "Client Credentials" flow. This will be a valuable resource for anyone looking to enhance their integration capabilities. I’m particularly interested in the potential use cases for the authorization code flow with IAS and XSUAA. Great job!

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello everyone! My name is Sandile Zikhali, and I am from South Africa. I am excited to express my interest in joining the SAP Skills Initiative. With my background as a Junior Accountant and a CIMA Candidate, I am eager to enhance my skills in SAP technologies and contribute to digital transformation in organizations. I want to pursue the SAP Certified Certified Associate - Business Process Integration with SAP S/4HANA - SAP S/4HANA certification, as it aligns perfectly with my career aspirations in financial management and implementation. Participating in this program will provide me with valuable insights and the opportunity to learn from experts while connecting with like-minded individuals in the SAP community. I look forward to this exciting journey!

@David_Chaviano I am willing to go through the rigors training that is required to obtain my certificate. The reason why I am asking to be considered for this opportunity is a matter of affordability. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 2 Contest Activities
entry.content: @vishalakshmi The Techtoberfest points/badge, like Petoberfest before it, has to be assigned manually.  All the fun friday activities are that way.  So it will be early next week before those are assigned.

entry.title: Hi Everyone
entry.content:
I have a strong interest in SAP due to its pivotal role in streamlining business processes and enhancing operational efficiency. My background in IT has equipped me with a solid foundation in systems analysis and exposure to software development, allowing me to appreciate the complexities and capabilities of enterprise resource planning.

Growing up in a remote area where technology is still advancing, I faced significant limitations in technology resources. This experience fueled my passion for leveraging technology to drive progress and innovation. I learned to adapt and find creative solutions with limited tools, which has strengthened my problem-solving skills and resourcefulness.

I believe my unique perspective, combined with my technical skills, makes me a good fit for this program. I am eager to deepen my understanding of SAP and contribute to projects that harness its power to make a meaningful impact, especially in under-resourced communities like my own. I am excited about the opportunity to learn from industry experts and collaborate with peers who share my passion for technology and its transformative potential.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Integration Suite Deploy Undeploy Config tool Part 2
entry.content: Hi @fatima4 - Nice blog. Would have been nice if all the iFlow steps are explained and also modularly designed. Also we can use OData adapter and avoid passing CSRF tokens etc? What is the reason of using HTTP adapter? How much time it takes to undeploy 200 iFlows at a time? We can use a timer step and that gest trigered automatically based on undeply schedule and avoid manually invoking throug postman? Also Iterating splitter causes more overhead due to independent executions for each split. General splitter is preferred?

entry.title: Re: 🟢 The superpower of CAP CDS: Calculated Elements and Annotation Expressions
entry.content: Amazing session!!

entry.title: Create CDS Entity in system 2 based on table in system 1
entry.content:
Hello All,

We are looking to create a CDS entity in system 2 based on data present in system 1. This entity in system 2 will be used by PaPM tool in system 1 for further processing, based on the following requirement:

In system 1, PaPM team are trying to build a solution, but the source data is present in system 2. Source data is too huge, and filters applied via PaPM in system 1 aren't working effectively. We are looking to create CDS entity in system 2, based on the filters maintained in system 1 (which is an ABAP table), so that PaPM can use the CDS created in system 2 as a source with less number of fields. 

We tried the following options, but they don't fit our requirement:

1. Custom entity and ABAP interface (Custom entity can't be used for data fetch and can be used for RAP framework only, so couldn't go further)

2. Entity with Virtual data elements (Entity doesn't reflect the values calculated via Virtual data elements, unless used in Odata/RAP, which doesn't meet our requirement.)

Is there any way to achieve this? 

I see one way is to create the filter table in system 2, and create CDS in system, but this requires efforts in building an Fiori app based on RAP framework (for user input), Odata (for excel upload) and UI5 coding.

Thanks & Regards,

Naga

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content: my vscode installations.

entry.title: Re: Error while posting MIRO Document.
entry.content:
Hi Kiran,

Since, our system configuration was perfectly alright. We had made changes to our "Message Control" Table, which solved our issue. And it was, as suggested by SAP.

SPRO Path : Logistics General -> Tax on Goods Movements - > India - > Tools -> Message Control

Change the Application Area : J_1IG_MSGS, Message No. 14 & 15, TCode: MIRO to "Warning".

Regards,

Chandan Barai

entry.title: Re: How to set Frame Options for Apps in Launchpad service?
entry.content: Have you tried whitelisting the url of the standalone approuter in the BTP subaccount where you have the Launchpad running? You can do this in the subaccount-> security-> settings

I am writing to express my keen interest in participating in the Digital Skills Initiative. With a background in operations, I have developed a strong foundation in Customer Service. My experience at Amazon has further honed my abilities and deepened my passion for leveraging technology to drive innovation.

 

I believe I am a good fit for this program because I am not only eager to expand my skills but also enthusiastic about collaborating with others to create impactful digital solutions. I am particularly drawn to hands-on projects and community engagement, and I am excited about the opportunity to contribute my unique perspective while learning from fellow participants and experts in the field.

 

Thank you for considering my application. I look forward to the possibility of being part of this transformative initiative.

 

Best regards,

Amey Ajmire

entry.title: SAP and Arm64 version of Windows 11 with Prism emulator (Snapdragon X series)
entry.content:
I'm wondering if anyone has experience with the new Snapdragon X series Windows 11 PCs. My expectation is that all new cloud solutions should work seamlessly through the browser, just like on x64 or x86 systems. However, for other tools such as SAP GUI, Java GUI, ADT (ABAP Development Tools), and even the OpenJDK for Windows on ARM64, the situation may be different. I understand that it's still early days, and some older solutions may never receive full support.

I'm also interested in how well the Prism emulator works for running SAP applications. I’m presuming that even if emulation adds a bit more than 20% processing overhead, it wouldn’t be too significant or even noticeable if everything functions as expected. The challenge is with the drivers and VPNs, which do not work under the Prism emulator.

Despite these concerns, there is already a substantial list of native software for Windows on ARM that’s available today. It will be interesting to see how the ecosystem continues to develop.

entry.title: Re: Preparing for Universal Parallel Accounting
entry.content:
This is very good detailed information and thanks for taking the time to put it together!  The note 3191636 you mentioned is fairly exhaustive. Although the note indicates it is being obsoleted as of August 2024, the successor note is not yet released so as far as I can tell this is the latest publicly available information.

I understand that the complete move to SAC (from classical planning transactions) and to event-based revenue recognition (from Results Analysis) is required, as well as needing to prioritize UPA over parallel COGM.

The parts that concerned me were under reporting, specifically the areas of product costing and material ledger (actual costing), e.g. below.  In addition it lists that project stock for engineering to order, CO production orders (and any reporting e.g KKBC_ORD) is not supported.

I'm not sure if you are the right person to update this, but it seems that there should be a description as to what the replacement is, for the items I outlined.  Otherwise even in a greenfield implementation, UPA may not be implemented as it seems to have many showstoppers for clients in manufacturing environments.

Please let me know if I am missing something (hopefully!)

 

Reporting

   

Detailed reports for material

 X

Not in scope:

  • Costed Multilevel BOM
  • Cost Components
  • Partner Cost Component Split
  • Cost Elements

Detailed reports for sales-order cost estimate and order BOM cost estimate

 X

Not in scope.

Reports for product cost by sales order

 X

Not in scope:

  • Sales Order Hierarchy Display
  • Display Sales Order Item to be Costed
  • Sales Order Selection
  • Analyze Sales Order

Detailed Reports

 

X

Not in scope:

  • Material Prices and Inventory Values Over Several Periods
  • Transaction History for Material
  • Valuated Multilevel Quantity Structure
  • Display of WIP for Actual Costs
  • WIP Quantity Document

entry.title: Key Highlights from the 2H 2024 Release of SuccessFactors Succession Planning
entry.content:
Hi Community,

The 2H 2024 release is here!

A new set of enhancements has been released in the 2H 2024 release that has been eagerly awaited. The SAP SuccessFactors 2H 2024 Release includes some key features and enhancements, so let's take a closer look!

1- Simplified Experience of Nominating Talent Pool Members to a Position:

For a position associated with talent pools, succession planners can now add successors directly from the talent pool sections on the position card in the Succession Org Chart or the Position Tile view.

Enablement: Automatically on

Previously, while working with a position card, the user had to navigate to the employee's talent card and start nomination from scratch, including searching for the position.
This feature has streamlined the way to add successors from talent pools in the Succession Org Chart or the Position Tile view.

Configuration Requirements:

Associated talent pools with positions through their underlying roles.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

Users have the User>Succession Planners>Succession Planning Permission permission. Otherwise, the + (Add as successor) button is not available in the talent pool sections of position cards.

For a detailed release summary, check out the “What’s New Viewer” for a list of enhancements across the application.

entry.title: Key Highlights from the 2H 2024 Release of SuccessFactors Calibration
entry.content:
Hi Community,

The 2H 2024 release is here!

A new set of enhancements has been released in the 2H 2024 release that has been eagerly awaited. The SAP SuccessFactors 2H 2024 Release includes some key features and enhancements, so let's take a closer look!

1- New Setting for Configuring Autosave:

In the last release Autosave feature was supported in the calibration and after 2H 2024 now, the admin can decide if they want to enable or disable the Autosave feature in calibration by changing the new setting, Enable Autosave, in the Manage Calibration Settings admin tool.

When the option is enabled, changes made in Calibration will be automatically saved after 30 seconds of inactivity. If disabled, users need to manually save any unsaved changes.

Enablement: Customer configured

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • Administrator>Manage Calibration>Manage Calibration Settings permission.

2- Person-Based Background Information Displayed for Concurrent Employment in Talent Card:

The talent card now displays background information for employees with concurrent employment based on persons rather than users.

When you've selected the option Enable Person-Based Background Information Blocks under Admin Center Company System and Logo Settings, all the background information records related to an employee with concurrent employments are displayed consistently across employments in the talent card. This data is the same as what appears on the People Profile.

Enablement: Automatically on

3- Set Numerical Distribution in Calibration Templates:

You can now set numerical distribution guidelines in Calibration templates for ratings from Performance Management forms. Previously, you could only define percentage-based distribution guidelines for ratings.

This feature allows you to specify the target number of subjects you want to assign to each rating. Here are the details:


  • You can configure both numerical and percentage-based guidelines for the same rating. The numerical guideline will take priority. If the number of subjects in a session matches any numerical guideline, it will be applied. Otherwise, if a percentage guideline exists, the percentage guideline will be applied instead; if no percentage guideline exists, no guideline will be applied.
  • You can set several numerical distribution guidelines for a single rating, but for the same total number of subjects, you can have only one guideline. For instance, if the total number of subjects is 5, and you already have the guideline "Excellent=1, Good=3, Fair=1", you can't create another guideline for this total number of 5. In other words, another guideline like "Excellent=2, Good=2, Fair=1" would not be allowed.
  • In each guideline, you can use operators = and ranges from...to.

You can set numerical distribution guidelines in the Data tab of the calibration template under Admin Center>Manage Calibration Templates.

When templates have identical rating types and scales, the configured distribution guidelines can be retained during template switching.

Here are just a few of the changes included in the latest release. For a detailed release summary, check out the “What’s New Viewer” for a list of enhancements across the application.

entry.title: DACH Academic Board 2024-2026 / Interview mit Prof. Mathis und Prof. Scheruhn
entry.content:
Sehr geehrte akademische Community aus Deutschland, Österreich und der Schweiz,

im Anschluss an die SAP Academic Community Conference 2024 im September in Wien möchte ich Ihnen im Interview mit Prof. Dr. Uta Mathis, lehrend an der Hochschule Esslingen, und Prof. Dr. Hans-Jürgen Scheruhn, lehrend an der Hochschule Harz, das neugewählte DACH Academic Board vorstellen, dem die beiden ab diesem Jahr gemeinsam vorstehen.

André Biener (AB): „Zum Einstieg würde mich interessieren: Welche Ziele hat sich das neue Academic Board gesetzt?“

Prof. Dr. Uta Mathis (UM): „Im ersten Schritt möchten wir mehr KollegInnen erreichen und das Interesse für die akademische Community stärken, damit diese deutlich aktiver wird. Der Austausch, während der SAP Academic Community Conference zeigte, dass ein großer Bedarf an Erfahrungsaustausch über neue Lehr- und Lernmethoden besteht. Dies möchten wir zentral in unsere Strategie für die kommende Zeit einfließen lassen, um sowohl neuen als auch erfahrenen KollegInnen die Möglichkeit zu geben, Erfahrungen zu teilen und neue Impulse aufzunehmen.

Darüber hinaus wollen wir die Studierenden stärker einbeziehen. Ihr Feedback ist uns wichtig und wir möchten sie besser über Lernmöglichkeiten informieren. Zudem planen wir, ein gemeinsames Forschungsnetzwerk aufzubauen und die Durchführung gemeinsamer Forschungsprojekte zu ermöglichen. Daher freuen wir uns, dass das Academic Board in diesem Jahr gewachsen ist. Abschließend möchten wir internationaler arbeiten, um den Umfang an potenziellen neuen Impulsen und Ansätzen zu erweitern.“

Prof. Scheruhn, Prof. Kilian (DACH Academic Board Head 2022-24), Prof. Mathis

Prof. Scheruhn, Prof. Kilian (DACH Academic Board Co-Lead 2022-24), Prof. Mathis

AB: „Um die gesteckten Ziele des Academic Boards zu erreichen, welche wesentlichen Punkte und Maßnahmen haltet ihr für besonders wichtig?“

UM: „Unsere KollegInnen im Board organisieren eine Vielzahl von herausragenden Events und Meetings, für die wir eine größere Sichtbarkeit schaffen möchten. Deshalb ist es für uns von zentraler Bedeutung, unsere mediale Präsenz zu verstärken und die Aktivität innerhalb der akademischen Community zu erhöhen.“

Prof. Dr. Hans-Jürgen Scheruhn (HS): „In diesen Formaten lässt sich aus unserer Sicht gut erkennen, dass viele Hochschulen noch nicht alle Potenziale der Digitalisierung im Bereich Lernen und Lehren voll ausschöpfen. Wir möchten die Hochschulen daher ermutigen bei der Einbindung von Lernenden und Lehrenden ähnliche Wege zu erproben bzw. einfach zu nutzen. Gleichzeitig wollen wir als Academic Board weiterhin Einfluss auf die SAP UA bei der wissenschaftlichen und didaktischen Aufbereitung der Inhalte nehmen.

Eine zunehmende Integration von BPM und ERP würde auch die Einbindung von Universitäten noch stärker vorantreiben, welche dadurch eine noch besser skalierbare Produktneutralität erlangen würden. Eine große Herausforderung ist auch zwei Jahre nach der Signavio Übernahme durch die SAP die aus unserer Sicht immer noch fehlende Integration von BPM und ERP in Forschung und Lehre. Diese wollen wir aktiv angehen und uns dabei auch in neue Formate stärker einbringen, wie z.B. in die SAP Business Process Open Lecture (BPOL).“

AB: „Ihr beiden seid bekannte Gesichter des Academic Boards. Was hat euch dazu bewegt, eine solche Rolle wahrzunehmen und dabei zu bleiben?“

HS:“ Das Academic Board ist eine wichtige von Lehrenden und der SAP gleichermaßen gewollte Einrichtung. Das Academic Board kann dadurch einerseits auf ein funktionierendes Ökosystem zurückgreifen und andererseits SAP-weit Einfluss auf die akademische Ausrichtung von aktuellen Themen wie z.B. der digitalen Transformation nehmen.“

UM: „Das Engagement und die Motivation der KollegInnen im Board sind unglaublich inspirierend. Es ist bereichernd, mit Gleichgesinnten zusammenzuarbeiten und einen fachlichen Austausch zu haben, der an der eigenen Hochschule nicht immer garantiert ist, sei es wegen isolierter Themen oder fakultätsübergreifender Barrieren. Alle im Board sind freiwillig dabei und hochmotiviert. Außerdem ist der Kontakt zu SAP und den UCCs inspirierend, da wir Informationen und Neuerungen aus erster Hand erhalten und die Möglichkeit haben, unsere Wünsche zu artikulieren und Angebote mitzugestalten.“

AB: „Nun mit einem Blick zu den inhaltlichen Schwerpunkten. Welchen Themenbereichen möchte das Academic Board in den kommenden Jahren fokussieren?“

UM: „Wir haben uns mehrere Schwerpunkte gesetzt. Zum einen möchten wir wie bereits erwähnt neue Lehr- und Lernmethoden sowie den Erfahrungsaustausch bei der Erstellung neuer Curricula fördern. Dabei sollen betriebswirtschaftliche und wissenschaftliche Themen und Methoden integriert werden.

Zum anderen sind es spezialisierte Fachbereiche wie Digitale Transformation und Management, neue Technologien wie Cloud-Technologien und Cyber-Sicherheit. Außerdem sind Themen wie Nachhaltigkeit und nachhaltiges Prozessmanagement von Bedeutung, ebenso wie die Methoden, Einsatzmöglichkeiten und Auswirkungen von Künstlicher Intelligenz auf Prozesse.“

HS: „Ein essenzieller Punkt ist für uns dabei der weitere Ausbau der Einbindung von SAP Signavio und LeanIX in die ERP-Lehre. Hier gilt es z.B. ein gegenüber den ERP-Systemen völlig andres Cloud-Nutzungskonzept für die Lernenden und Lehrenden im Bereich BPM weiter zu festigen und z.B.  bewährte Features der Customer edition in SAP Signavio weiterhin auch in der sogenannten Academic edition zur Verfügung zu stellen. Der verstärkte Einsatz von SAP EnableNow ermöglicht aus unserer Sicht völlig neue didaktische Ansätze in der BPM- und ERP-Lehre.“

AB: „Nachhaltigkeit und Künstliche Intelligenz sind die großen Themen der aktuellen Zeit. Wie wurden diese in die künftige Arbeit des Boards integriert?“

UM: „Nachhaltigkeit und KI sind für uns Querschnittsthemen, weil diese in jedes Thema hineingehören. Deshalb haben wir auch keine Arbeitsgruppen, die sich ausschließlich mit den Themen beschäftigen, sondern in jeder Arbeitsgruppe wird das Thema entsprechend mit adressiert und integriert.“

AB: „Als SAP und Ihr als Vertreter aus dem Academic Board arbeiten wir auch gemeinsam. Wie gestaltet sich dies aus eurer Perspektive?“

HS: „Die Zusammenarbeit mit SAP ist aus Sicht des Academic Board sehr angenehm und von großem gegenseitigen Respekt geprägt. Dabei unterstützt SAP die stetige Entwicklung des Academic Board DACH zu einer Art Drehscheibe zwischen den weiteren 4 regionalen Boards. In der Gruppe BPM bieten wir zu den oben adressierten Themen seit über 3 Jahren 2-3 mal jährlich weltweit Train-the-Trainer Kurse für Lehrende der SAP University Alliances in einem digitalen Format an. Diese Kurse werden aufgezeichnet und allen angemeldeten Lehrenden anschließend zur Verfügung gestellt. Durch die Verlegung der halbtägigen Veranstaltungen in die frühen Abendstunden können wir erfolgreich unterschiedliche Zeitzonen adressieren. Dabei erhalten die Teilnehmenden unter anderem Zugang zu ca. 10 verschiedenen Learning Journeys aus den oben genannten Themenbereichen. Auch in die Umsetzung der SAP BPOL bringen wir uns didaktisch und inhaltlich wissenschaftlich ein. Auf der nächsten SAP ACC2025 in Potsdam und Berlin wollen wir als Academic Board das Thema Integration BPM und ERP fokussieren und dabei erneut die Ideen der teilnehmenden Hochschulen in wissenschaftlichen und praktischen Beiträgen auf großer Bühne in Szene setzen.“

UM: „Ich schätze die Zusammenarbeit mit der SAP sehr. Mit Dir Andre als regional-zuständiger Mitarbeiter der University Alliances haben wir einen direkten Ansprechpartner. Gemeinsam setzen wir uns in monatlichen virtuellen Meetings zusammen. Großen Dank auch an das Team für den Aufbau der Plattform zum Dokumentenaustausch.

Durch die Zusammenarbeit ist es für uns deutlich eine Erleichterung mit Experten aus der Wirtschaft in Kontakt zu kommen und auch die internationale Präsenz von SAP kommt uns bei dem Bestreben entgegen, internationalen Austausch zu verstärken. Ich habe mich zu jeder Zeit als Teil der akademischen Community wertgeschätzt gefühlt und auch in der Führungsebene der SAP und University Alliances wird die Bedeutung der Zusammenarbeit mitgetragen.“

AB: „Welche Erfahrungen konntet ihr bereits sammeln im Austausch innerhalb der Community, die euch positiv in Erinnerung geblieben sind?“

UM: „Ich selbst habe einmal eine Fallstudie erstellt. Die Inspiration dazu kam durch andere Fallstudien die andere KollegInnen einst auf der SAP Academic Community Conference vorgestellt hatten. Indem wir dies fortwährend praktizieren, unseren Fortschritt für die akademische Community bereitstellen, können wir uns nicht nur gegenseitig Impulse geben, sondern eventuell können Ergebnisse und Erkenntnisse für weitere Projekte verwendet werden.

In meinem Bestreben internationalen Austausch zu fördern, kommt es mir Zugute, dass ich an meiner Einrichtung Auslandsbeauftragte bin. Hier versuche ich im besten Fall mich mit Einrichtungen zu vernetzen, die Partnerschulen sind und auch in der akademischen Community aktiv sind. Im ersten Schritt strebe ich ein Co-Teaching an.“

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content:

my office setup at work:

KR

Michael

entry.title: Free SAP Build apps on course
entry.content:
Hi,

I am new on this platform. I am interested in learning SAP Build Apps and at the end of the course would like to get certified at the end of the course by taking the examination.

I will truly appreciate it if links for free online SAP Build Apps course for certification can be provided. 

Regards

entry.title: Re: 🇦🇹 SAP Build: Create Business Apps and Processes Without Code (Vienna, Austria)
entry.content: I agree with Franz. We have some introductions, then a short presentation about SAP Build. If you are ready with the prerequisites before you come, you won't miss much.

entry.title: From ECC to S/4HANA Public Cloud Refactor (“Re-architect”)
entry.content:
Dear Architects,

Currently I am working for customer which has ABAP code written for ast 20 years and they created there own application in ABAP. Now same sales guru told them you can choose S/4HANA public cloud as new system instead of choosing private cloud.

Now I need to make strategy for this custom code as not  only 30 % can be covered by fit to standard. I am proposing to build new Extension using CAPM with PostgreSQL and may be using SAP Build Pro Code with Joule to accelerate and help development.

What is you opinion about my approach plus is there any advantage to got for RAP which I cannot find any(code reusability is minimum from old ABAP to RAP`)?  

Thanks in advance.

Best Regards,

ebsor

entry.title: Re: 🇦🇹 SAP Build: Create Business Apps and Processes Without Code (Vienna, Austria)
entry.content: There was a time for Process Automation there was an issue with Edge, but that is mostly/completely related to automation bots, which we will not create. As Franz quoted above, you should be fine.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
Re: What is Composable Architecture?
entry.content: Thanks @ShaikAzmathulla

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano ,

My name is Ismael. I'm from middle east. I currently work in the Human Resources field and looking for opportunities to strengthen my digital skills to pursue a career in Instructional Design. I am passionate about learning and development and SAP offers a variety of learning journeys and certification to fulfill my professional development. I look forward to participating in this initiative and hope that I can make an impact on learning for many organizations.

Thank you for this wonderful opportunity,

entry.title: City of Portland (Oregon) SAP Technical Manager (Information Systems Manager I) Opportunity
entry.content:
City of Portland

SAP Technical Manager (Information Systems Manager I), 2024-01005

Job Appointment:  Regular, Full time

Work Schedule:  Monday – Friday, 8am-5pm.  Alternate schedule may be available.

Work Location:  This position has an exception to work primarily remote. However, there will be instances when it is necessary to come onsite for work (with advance notice).  Remote work must be performed within Oregon or Washington. For more information, click here.

Benefits:  Please check our benefit tab for an overview of benefit for this position

Application Material: Please click APPLY to submit your application via the City of Portland’s online portal. You will need to attach a cover letter and resume.

About the Position:
Join the City of Portland in a key strategic role as the SAP Technical Manager (Information Systems Manager I) for the Enterprise Business Solutions (EBS) team. This position offers a unique opportunity to strategically continue leading the transformation from our existing platform to the next SAP generation to provide a citywide platform through innovative technology solutions. Working with a dedicated team and across the city, you will be at the forefront of creating meaningful change and transition to best-in-class SAP cloud technology and leveraging your expertise to provide the system of record for all the city's Human Capital Management and Financial Management activities. 

For more information and to apply, please visit: SAP Technical Manager (Information Systems Manager I) Job Announcement

entry.title: Teamlead (m/w/d) Consulting SAP S/4HANA Sourcing & Procurement
entry.content:
Teamlead (m/w/d) Consulting SAP S/4HANA Sourcing & Procurement - Standort: Heilbronn

Als Führungspersönlichkeit arbeitest Du gemeinsam mit Deinem Team an der erfolgreichen Umsetzung Eurer Kundenprojekte. Im Rahmen von S/4HANA Greenfield Einführung übernimmst Du die Rolle des Streamleads oder des Project Manager (m/w/d).

Gemeinsam mit dem Vertrieb planst Du die Entwicklung Deines Teams auf Basis konkreter Opportunities und gewinnst zukünftige Projekte. Der Umgang mit Menschen und die Weiterentwicklung Deiner Mitarbeiter:innen liegen Dir am Herzen.

Deine Aufgaben:

  • Projektplanung und Projektleitung mittels JIRA
  • Aufgabenzuweisung und Unterstützung Deiner Mitarbeiter:innen im Projekt
  • Sparringspartner des Fachbereichs zu Vertriebs- und Beschaffungsprozessen
  • Aktive Mitarbeit in S/4 HANA Projekten für SD/MM Prozesse.
  • Erstellung der technischen Kundenangebote für Order2Cash und Source2Pay Prozesse
  • Angebotspräsentation und aktive Zuarbeit für den Vertrieb
  • Ausbau deines Teams durch Zusammenarbeit mit dem Recruitment und Deiner eigenen Recruitingaktivitäten sowie Entwicklung Deiner Teammitglieder
  • Weiterentwicklung Deines Beratungsportfolios mit konkreten Lösungsangeboten und Präsentation in unterschiedlichsten Medien

Erkennst Du Dich wieder?

  • Persönlichkeit: Kommunikationsstark, lösungsorientiert, kreativ, durchsetzungsfähig sowie hohes Qualitätsbewusstsein und schnelle Auffassungsgabe
  • Arbeitsweise: Emphatisch, strategisch denkend, ziel- und ergebnisorientiert
  • Erfahrungen: Berufserfahrung als Projektleiter:in oder Streamlead mit SAP Vertriebsprozesse oder Beschaffung. Idealerweise erste Führungserfahrung
  • Begeisterung: Freude am Umgang mit Menschen und Fähigkeit diese von neuesten Technologien und Trends in O2C oder S2P zu begeistern
  • Netzwerk: Gut im Netzwerken sowie Fähigkeit, andere zu begeistern
  • Know-How: Gutes Prozesswissen zu Vertriebs- und Einkaufsprozessen mit Umsetzungserfahrung in SAP SD/MM
  • Sprachen: Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift

Warum IBsolution?

  • Persönlich auf Dich zugeschnittene Weiterbildungsmöglichkeiten und ein umfassendes Onboarding Programm, welches Deinen Einstieg bei uns erleichtert
  • 30+ Tage Urlaub & Sonderurlaub für Deine Weiterbildung
  • Mobiles Arbeiten zur flexibleren Gestaltung Deines Arbeitsalltags und Privatlebens sowie eine ausgewogene Work-Life-Balance
  • Mittagsessenzuschuss, freie Getränkeauswahl und frisches Obst für Deine Gesundheit
  • Freiraum für aktives Treiben innovativer Themen und Mitgestaltung
  • Eine außergewöhnliche Arbeitsatmosphäre und Teamspirit in einem wertschätzenden und chancenreichen Arbeitsumfeld mit kurzen Entscheidungswegen
  • Arbeiten mit den neuesten SAP-Technologien
  • Zusätzlich darfst Du Dich auf viele Firmenbenefits wie einer Mitgliedschaft bei Urban Sports oder ausgefallene Mitarbeiterevents und viele weitere Benefits freuen!

Bewirb Dich jetzt Jobs: Teamlead (m/w/d) Consulting SAP S/4HANA Sourcing & Procurement in Heilbronn | IBsolution GmbH

Wir freuen uns auf Dich!

entry.title: Solution Architect (m/w/d) - Cloud SAP Business Technology Platform
entry.content:
Solution Architect (m/w/d) - Cloud SAP Business Technology Platform
- Remote

Deine Aufgaben:

  • Verantwortung für Plattformentscheidung, Architektur, technisches Design, Integrationstechnologie und deren Optimierung unter Einsatz neuester Technologien
  • Analyse der fachlichen und technischen Anforderungen unserer Kunden sowie Design von Lösung der anspruchsvollen Kundenbedürfnisse
  • Fachliche Steuerung und Weiterentwicklung der Projektteams in den neuesten Technologien
  • Setzen wichtiger Impulse und Ideen für nachhaltige Lösungen, die die modernsten und neuesten Aspekte berücksichtigen
  • Verantwortung für die qualitative Umsetzung und die notwendigen Anpassungen des Konzepts innerhalb der Implementierung
  • Sicherstellung der termingerechten Übergabe der Entwicklungsleistungen in die verschiedenen Sprints, Projektphasen oder in den Betrieb
  • Verantwortung für alle Integrations- und Entwicklungsthemen

Erkennst Du Dich wieder?

  • Persönlichkeit: Teamfähig, eigenverantwortlich und -initiativ, hohes Qualitätsbewusstsein, wissbegierig
  • Arbeitsweise: Strukturiert, analytische und schnelle Auffassungsgabe, lösungsorientiert, verantwortungsbewusst
  • Erfahrungen: Mindestens vier Jahre Projekterfahrung im Cloud-Umfeld, speziell auf der SAP Business Technology Plattform
  • Begeisterung: Technische Lösungen für knifflige Probleme finden, Spaß an neuen Themen (z. B. Machine Learning, Digital Twin, Native Web Development)
  • Know-How: Praktische Erfahrung in mindestens einem der folgenden Bereiche: SAP Extension Suite, SAP Integration Suite, SAP Fiori / UI5, Cloud Application Programming Modell, Node.js, React.js
  • Sprachen: Gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift
  • Ausbildung: (Fach-)Hochschulabschluss, bevorzugt Informatik/ Wirtschaftsinformatik oder aus einem anderen naturwissenschaftlichen Gebiet

Warum IBsolution?

  • Arbeiten mit den neuesten SAP- und Cloud-Technologien 
  • Umfassendes Onboarding Programm welches Deinen Einstieg bei uns erleichtert
  • Unser Mindset: Familiäres Miteinander und Teamspirit in einem und chancenreichen Arbeitsumfeld
  • Dank maßgeschneiderter Weiterbildungsprogramme bringen wir Dich in Deiner beruflichen und persönlichen Entwicklung einen großen Schritt voran
  • Viele weitere attraktive Benefits

Bewirb Dich jetzt Jobs: Solution Architect (m/w/d) - Cloud- SAP Business Technology Platform in Remote | IBsolution GmbH

Wir freuen uns auf Dich!

entry.title: Teamlead (m/w/d) Consulting SAP Supply Chain Management
entry.content:
Teamlead (m/w/d) Consulting SAP Supply Chain Management - Standort: Heilbronn

Als Führungspersönlichkeit arbeitest Du gemeinsam mit Deinem Team an der erfolgreichen Umsetzung Eurer Kundenprojekte. Im Rahmen von S/4HANA Greenfield Einführung übernimmst Du die Rolle des Streamleads oder des Project Manager (m/w/d).

Gemeinsam mit dem Vertrieb planst Du die Entwicklung Deines Teams auf Basis konkreter Opportunities und gewinnst zukünftige Projekte. Der Umgang mit Menschen und die Weiterentwicklung Deiner Mitarbeiter:innen liegen Dir am Herzen.

Deine Aufgaben:

  • Aufbau des Beratungsteams Supply Chain Management durch Zusammenarbeit mit dem Recruitment und Deiner eigenen Recruitingaktivitäten sowie Entwicklung Deiner Teammitglieder
  • Erstellung der technischen Kundenangebote für Prozesse um Fertigungsplanung und Fertigungssteuerung
  • Angebotspräsentation und aktive Zuarbeit für den Vertrieb
  • Projektplanung und Projektleitung mittels JIRA
  • Aufgabenzuweisung und Unterstützung Deiner Mitarbeiter:innen im Projekt
  • Sparringspartner des Fachbereichs zu Prozesse um Servicemanagement und Asset Management
  • Aktive Mitarbeit in Projekten für S/4 HANA PP und SAP IBP
  • Weiterentwicklung Deines Beratungsportfolios mit konkreten Lösungsangeboten und Präsentationen in unterschiedlichsten Medien

Erkennst Du Dich wieder?

  • Persönlichkeit: Kommunikationsstark, lösungsorientiert, kreativ, durchsetzungsfähig sowie hohes Qualitätsbewusstsein und schnelle Auffassungsgabe
  • Arbeitsweise: Emphatisch, strategisch denkend, ziel- und ergebnisorientiert
  • Erfahrungen: Berufserfahrung als Projektleiter:in oder Streamlead mit SAP in Fertigungsprozessen, idealerweise mit erster Führungserfahrung
  • Begeisterung: Freude am Umgang mit Menschen und Fähigkeit diese von neuesten Technologien und Trends im Supply Chain Management zu begeistern
  • Netzwerk: Gut im Netzwerken und darin, andere zu begeistern ihm/ihr zu folgen
  • Know-How: Gutes Prozesswissen zu Fertigung in verschiedenen Branchen mit Umsetzungserfahrung in SAP PP, SAP APO oder SAP IBP
  • Sprachen: Sehr gute Deutsch- & Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift

Warum IBsolution?

  • Wachstumsstark in dem Du Deine Karriere schnell entwickeln kannst
  • Klarer Verantwortungsrahmen in dem Du entscheidest
  • SAP BTP Partner of the year – Arbeiten am Puls der Zeit mit den neuesten SAP-Technologien
  • Mobiles Arbeiten, damit Du Deinen Arbeitsalltag und Dein Privatleben flexibler gestalten und besser vereinen kannst
  • Persönlich auf Dich zugeschnittene Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten und ein umfassendes Onboarding Programm welches Deinen Einstieg bei uns erleichtert.
  • Du erhältst 30+ Tage Urlaub & Sonderurlaub für Deine Weiterbildung
  • Familiäres Miteinander und Teamspirit in einem wertschätzenden und chancenreichen Arbeitsumfeld
  • Zusätzlich darfst Du Dich auf viele Firmenbenefits wie einer Mitgliedschaft bei Urban Sports oder ausgefallene Mitarbeiterevents und viele weitere Benefits freuen!

Bewirb Dich jetzt Jobs: Teamlead (m/w/d) Consulting SAP Supply Chain Management in Heilbronn | IBsolution GmbH

Wir freuen uns auf Dich!

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content:
Hi, 

this is my personal working setup in my home. I use ADT and (sometimes still) SE80 as development tools for may daily work. I am used to have 2 screens with a size auf 27" each, instead of one large screen. I like to have some green (more or less :D) plants around me and a Coke to stay awake when working in the evening 🙂

 

Kind regards
Jens

entry.title: Re: I am unable to log in with SAP* or DDIC user passwords
entry.content:
@Sandra_Rossi,

login/no_automatic_user_sapstar need to be disabled to log in as sap*

If a sap* user was created manually then that password is required to log in - the default password will not work

 

The SQL error is due to the the user executing the SQL command  not having sufficient permission at the database level - it's not SAP related

entry.title: [FSCM Collections]: Extend Manage Customer Line Items(F0711)– OData MPC & DPC Code Enhancement – V20
entry.content:
INTRODUCTION:

We have been given a requirement to add a set of custom fields to be extended in Manage Customer Line Items (App ID F0711) – Credit Management.

Challenge: The current system is an S4 HANA system version 2021, where CFL (Custom Fields and Logic) has been introduced but isn't available for the required App. Given is a SEGW Project with OData Services based on a Mapped Data Source (MDS). Redefinition w/ Extension of the project isn't possible, and we should not Overwrite the Std. Project.

Solution (Not a Clean-Core 😞) -This app is an OData Service application, so enhancing the MPC & DPC will be able to view the fields but can’t sort & filter the list as they are not part of the standard mapped CDS view and can’t be handled in SADL query options.

In this blog post, we will cover the required steps:

  • Extend the DDIC Structure, CDS View (if any) – Database Layer
  • Enhance the Service MPC (Model) – w/ BADI or Implicit (not recommended) Enhancement
  • Enhance the Service DPC (Data) – w/ BADI or Implicit (not recommended) Enhancement

to handle the requirement.

 

MAIN CONTENT:

  1. From the Fiori Application F12 or Fiori Library, get the service name to open the respective SEGW Project and look for the Responsible Entity. You can check the Mapping for Ease

     Open the DDIC Structure and look for the Persistent/Transient Include for our required, but here we found SQL Structure for CDS View itself.

     So, let's better extend the required custom fields in there with the help of an APPEND structure.

  2. Let’s try to find some BADI with Enhancement Spots first for OData, which we didn’t find for our App scenario. Hence, proceeded with Implicit Enhancement at MPC_EXT – Method DEFINE.

 

 

DATA: lo_entity_type TYPE REF TO /iwbep/if_mgw_odata_entity_typ, "#EC NEEDED
      lo_property    TYPE REF TO /iwbep/if_mgw_odata_property, "#EC NEEDED
      lv_text_ele    TYPE textpoolky,
      lv_char        TYPE i.

" Map the Entity Type
  lo_entity_type = model->get_entity_type( iv_entity_name = 'Item'  ).

" Loop For no of Custom fields
DO 2 TIMES.                                            "#EC CI_NESTED
    IF sy-index = 1.
      lo_entity_type->create_property( 
                    EXPORTING 
                      iv_property_name  = 'CollectionNotes1'
                      iv_abap_fieldname = 'ZZ_COMMENT1'
                    RECEIVING 
                      ro_property = DATA(lo_pro_item1) ). " LO_PROPERTY
      lv_text_ele = |CN1| . " Literal for Text-Element
    ELSE.
      lo_entity_type->create_property( 
                    EXPORTING 
                      iv_property_name  = 'CollectionNotes2'
                      iv_abap_fieldname = 'ZZ_COMMENT2'
                    RECEIVING 
                      ro_property = lo_pro_item1 ). " LO_PROPERTY
      lv_text_ele = |CN2| .
    ENDIF.

    lv_char = 128.

" Create the Property Details
    lo_pro_item1->set_type_edm_string( ).
    lo_pro_item1->set_maxlength( lv_char ).
    lo_pro_item->set_nullable( abap_true ).
    lo_pro_item1->set_updatable( abap_true ).
    lo_pro_item1->set_sortable( abap_false ). 
    lo_pro_item1->set_filterable( abap_false ).

" When to Map Front-End Labels different from ABAP field's DATA ELEMENT.
" Create Text CN1 & CN2 in Custom Class
    lo_pro_item1->set_label_from_text_element(
      EXPORTING
        iv_text_element_symbol = lv_text_ele
        iv_text_element_container zfscmcl_customer_line_comment=>gc_class_name " Constant - Custom Class Name 
**         io_object_ref          = me  " when Text-elements of MPC_EXT is used
        ).
    lo_pro_item1->set_internal_type( 'C' ).
    lo_pro_item1->set_internal_length( lv_char ). "( '128' ).
    CLEAR: lv_text_ele.
  ENDDO.

cl_afle_mpc_ext_utility_class=>adjust_model( model ). "AFLE​

 

 

 

Adjusting the Model is necessary to handle action redefinition/addition. The front end can be handled by the Extension/Adaptation Project (if needed).

 

  • All the Data handling for the enhanced fields must be handled in this class methods to related entities & entity sets. Now Field values can be database stored or Run-time calculated. Here, Values are fetched at the Item level first. Values for multiple or single records have been fetched. So, create Enhancement at ITEMSET_GET_ENTITYSET:

 

 

************************************************************************
"Create Key Field Structure in Custom Class
  types:
    BEGIN OF gty_cli_key ,
        bukrs TYPE bukrs,
        belnr TYPE belnr_d,
        gjahr TYPE gjahr,
        buzei TYPE buzei,
        kunnr TYPE kunnr,
      END OF gty_cli_key .
  types:
    gtt_cli_key TYPE SORTED TABLE OF gty_cli_key WITH UNIQUE KEY bukrs belnr gjahr buzei .

************************************************************************
" In GET_ENTITYSET
    lt_cli_key = VALUE #( FOR ls_entity IN et_entityset 
                        WHERE ( companycode IS NOT INITIAL "#EC CI_STDSEQ
                                AND accountingdocument IS NOT INITIAL )
                              ( bukrs = ls_entity-companycode
                                belnr = ls_entity-accountingdocument
                                buzei = ls_entity-accountingdocumentitem
                                gjahr = ls_entity-fiscalyear )
                         ).
 
    IF lt_cli_key IS NOT INITIAL.
"Custom Method to process the data.
      DATA(lt_comments) = zfscmcl_customer_line_comment=>get_comments(  
                               it_cli_key = lt_cli_key ).
      UNASSIGN <ls_entity>.
      LOOP AT lt_comments ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_comments>).
        ASSIGN et_entityset[ 
                     companycode = <ls_comments>-company_code "#EC CI_STDSEQ
                     accountingdocument     = <ls_comments>-document_number
                     accountingdocumentitem = <ls_comments>-line_item_number
                     fiscalyear             = <ls_comments>-fiscal_year     
                   ] TO <ls_entity>.
        
          IF <ls_entity> IS ASSIGNED.
            <ls_entity>-zz_comment1 = <ls_comments>-comment1.
            <ls_entity>-zz_comment2 = <ls_comments>-comment2.
            UNASSIGN <ls_entity>.
           ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.
      IF <ls_comments> IS ASSIGNED. UNASSIGN <ls_comments>. ENDIF.
      CLEAR: lt_comments.
    ENDIF.

 

 

4. Now check the fields in Adapt Settings. Better Clear Caches & Refresh Service Metdata with: 

  • IWBEP/CLEAN_CACHE
  • IWFND/CLEAN_CACHE
  • Refresh Metadata Layer at IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE

entry.title: Re: Universal ID Problem with Linked Accounts
entry.content: hi @anisen, Can you share, how we can proceed to schedule??

entry.title: Re: Universal ID Problem with Linked Accounts
entry.content:
I am still stuck at this page, can anyone tell me how to proceed further??

I have two accounts in Linked - One personal with email & P User ID and other SAP one with sap email & I User ID.
@audreystevenson @anisen @AtulT115

entry.title: Re: Cannot run "Run Data Mapping" in QS4
entry.content: GRDC is an SaaS solution, the possibility of bug to cause job not scheduled is quite low. It might be the configuration on BTP, but no harm to create customer incident.

entry.title: Re: Data Monitor-Reported data Validation rules-Comments
entry.content: As long as the data is stored in database without encryption, you could be able to achieve this. Please explore the tables starting from VEC. The validation is cross components which means also works for FI in addition to Group Reporting.

entry.title: Re: Error No Memory Available in Preparation for Consolidation Group Changes / PCC
entry.content: Total of 24GB memory for a work process is a little bit large. The code line in the dump occurred might not be the root cause, maybe create an incident with SAP to further check.

entry.title: Re: Gropup reporting on premise 2023/SP1 - enable the hie...
entry.content: the master cds view only handle the hierarchy within the same table via HRRP*. So the node is text rather than other dimension such as project -> WBS. In a word, level based hierarchy or external hierarchy ( BW terminology ) is not supported in this case.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
I am writing to express my interest in this SAP Digital Skills Initiative Program. I  fall under the category of "People looking to switch their careers mid-life". 

I have gained experience in Logistics, IT, Project Management, Retail and Transport which supports that I am best candidature for this role. I am well disciplined, structured, honest, and energetic individual thriving hard for uphold the values of quality, agility, and professionalism. During this Experience I regularly collaborated with all team members, range of customers to work with strict deadlines, maintain accuracy and quality, effectiveness and minimal risk.

From this experience I have developed an understanding of processes, procedures and business insights, requirements and more. I have developed many skills during my education and experience, and I believe I have abundance of transferable skills that can make me a valuable professional in SAP Digital Industry.

I have attained Masters degree in Manufacturing and Diploma of Project Management. I aim develop as a SAP Consultant using this Digital Skills Initiative Program. 

Hence my request to all relevant stakeholders in this learning journey to provide support and guidance to proceed and succeed. 

 

Thanks 

Satish

entry.title: Re: Employee Central Mass Data Management for Position
entry.content: We have also implemented this tool, keen to also understand if SAP will be adding additional attributes like Parent Position as per the last post.

entry.title: Re: Video Series - Part 10 - Testing CAP application through Postman
entry.content:
Hi @JeyakumarAsai,

This sounds to me as if you would have an issue with your environment variable APP_URL. My first thoughts would be: is your app server up and running? In case answer is Yes, I would review the video where I am showing how to get the right URL (around minute 5:30). 

Hope this gives you some hints.

Regards.

Alejandro.

entry.title: New Member Intro
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

 

My name is Tiffany and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community! A little about myself: I have years of front facing customer service experience, many more years working with computers. I’m very curious and spend a lot of time learning about various things that interest me. I also enjoy socializing, networking and traveling from weekend road trips to abroad.

 

I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m really looking forward to expanding my knowledge in supply chain.

 

I’m particularly excited about obtaining this certification because I’m looking to expand beyond IT help desk, and really explore SAP Cloud

 

I’m also open to connecting with others who are interested in cloud and AI solutions for the supply chain industry.

Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!

 

Best regards,  

Tiffany Christman

entry.title: Re: Hi
entry.content: Morning to u all

entry.title: Re: Hi
entry.content: Hi

entry.title: Re: Hi
entry.content: Am here

entry.title: Re: BRIM-The subscriber account "00000*****" (with "BRIM"...
entry.content: I am getting similar error. Is there any way to fix the issue?
